opener.dateline:
Legation of the United States,
Mexico, July 29, 1890. (Received July 30.)

div.p: Mr. Ryan reports that he has seen a telegram just received from Ezeta to his special agent in Mexico in relation to the suppression of telegrams, and which reads: “I have ordered telegrams of Mexican and American Governments to be passed, but lines in Guatemala are broken. A ship might be gotten by the American minister to transport his communications to San José.”
